description: A scheduling request — draft should propose a concrete time or commit to one of the offered slots.

email.subject: Quick call next week?
email.snippet: Could we grab 30 minutes next week to catch up on the integration work?
email.body:
Hey,

Hope you're doing well. Could we grab 30 minutes next week to catch up on the integration work? Tuesday or Wednesday afternoon would be great on my end.

Frank

rubric:
A good draft for this email:
- Picks one of the offered slots (Tuesday or Wednesday afternoon) OR proposes a specific alternative time
- Confirms the 30-minute duration
- Mentions the integration work in some form (not a generic 'happy to chat')
- Conversational tone appropriate for a customer relationship
- Does NOT defer to an assistant (EA is disabled in this fixture)
- Does NOT propose vague things like 'next week works'

Bad outputs:
- Vague 'sounds good, send me a time' — defeats the purpose since Frank already offered times
- Over-formal corporate-speak for what should be a friendly reply
- Ignoring the integration-work context
